segments.text: In this video, we will demonstrate how
to set up the Sona Cube MCP server in
Cursor. You will need to be in connected
mode to either Sonar Cube cloud or
server.
We start in my cursor editor and head to
my Sona Cube view. Notice I'm in
connected mode. I can actually configure
my MCP server here
or I can head to the AI agents
configuration window and click the
button.
This starts the configuration process.
When it's finished, you will see the
Sonar Cube MCP tools enabled.
Then head to the agent dialogue box
where you can begin to instruct the
agent. In this case, I'll ask it to
search for security vulnerabilities in
my project.
And off it goes to work on the task
using the tools the SonoCube MCP server
has at its disposal.
It will then report back with what it
has found and provide its
recommendations.
In this example, we then ask it to fix
the most important vulnerability. And
off it goes.
And there you are, all set with the
SonarCube MCP server installed and ready
for your instructions.
If you're ready to dive deeper, head
over to our documentation. I've dropped
a link in the description.
Happy coding.

fullText: In this video, we will demonstrate how to set up the Sona Cube MCP server in Cursor. You will need to be in connected mode to either Sonar Cube cloud or server. We start in my cursor editor and head to my Sona Cube view. Notice I'm in connected mode. I can actually configure my MCP server here or I can head to the AI agents configuration window and click the button. This starts the configuration process. When it's finished, you will see the Sonar Cube MCP tools enabled. Then head to the agent dialogue box where you can begin to instruct the agent. In this case, I'll ask it to search for security vulnerabilities in my project. And off it goes to work on the task using the tools the SonoCube MCP server has at its disposal. It will then report back with what it has found and provide its recommendations. In this example, we then ask it to fix the most important vulnerability. And off it goes. And there you are, all set with the SonarCube MCP server installed and ready for your instructions. If you're ready to dive deeper, head over to our documentation. I've dropped a link in the description. Happy coding.
